symptoms.symptom_name: Musty / mousy odour of breath and skin

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute phenylalanine neurotoxicity (metabolic decompensation of PKU)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The musty/mousy odour reflects phenylketone (phenylacetate) excretion at markedly elevated phenylalanine levels — near-specific in a known PKU patient.
Stimulant intoxication does not produce this characteristic metabolic odour.

symptoms.symptom_name: New continuous bilateral resting hand tremor
symptoms.character: continuous, fine, present at rest, both hands

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute phenylalanine neurotoxicity (metabolic decompensation of PKU)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute extrapyramidal tremor fits acute CNS neurotoxicity in the metabolic context.
Acute onset over hours with hyperreflexia is not consistent with a chronic benign tremor.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute phenylalanine neurotoxicity (metabolic decompensation of PKU)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Neurotoxic phenylalanine impairs neurotransmitter synthesis, producing agitation and insomnia.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Primary bipolar mania / acute psychiatric episode
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Agitation and insomnia are non-specific and also occur in primary psychiatric illness — not discriminating on their own.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute phenylalanine neurotoxicity (metabolic decompensation of PKU)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective CNS irritability supporting a neurotoxic/metabolic process; clinician-only, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intermittent dystonic posturing of the hands

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute phenylalanine neurotoxicity (metabolic decompensation of PKU)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Extrapyramidal dystonia adds to the picture of acute basal-ganglia-level neurotoxicity.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypopigmentation — very fair skin, blonde hair, blue eyes

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute phenylalanine neurotoxicity (metabolic decompensation of PKU)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic hypopigmentation from reduced tyrosine/melanin synthesis is a background marker of PKU, not of the acute crisis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Flared eczematous rash on flexor surfaces

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A comorbid eczema flare, a secondary irritant worsening his distress; not diagnostic of the metabolic crisis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (heart rate ~118) with mildly elevated blood pressure

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute phenylalanine neurotoxicity (metabolic decompensation of PKU)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sympathetic activation from severe agitation; non-specific and clinician-measured, sealed from telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sustained dietary non-compliance — refusal of formula, intake of normal protein for ~2 weeks

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute phenylalanine neurotoxicity (metabolic decompensation of PKU)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The direct, reversible cause of the toxic phenylalanine accumulation.

investigation_details.expected_result: Markedly elevated above the therapeutic target range, confirming neurotoxic accumulation
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The severity of the decompensation cannot be quantified and safe management/monitoring cannot be guided.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute phenylalanine neurotoxicity (metabolic decompensation of PKU)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A markedly elevated phenylalanine level is the definitive confirmation — but it is a laboratory test, never available in real time via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute phenylalanine neurotoxicity (metabolic decompensation of PKU)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever argues against a primary infective/septic encephalopathy but does not confirm the metabolic cause.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute phenylalanine neurotoxicity (metabolic decompensation of PKU)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The musty odour, new tremor and dystonia, hyperreflexia, escalating agitation and sustained dietary non-compliance together point specifically to acute metabolic neurotoxicity rather than a psychiatric or drug cause.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies the neurotoxic accumulation and guides the metabolic washout and monitoring — the single most important test.
Characterises the metabolic derangement and supports specialist management.
Reasonable to exclude stimulant use given the agitation, but must not delay metabolic assessment; the metabolic cause is clear and takes priority.
